L1.area: Authentication credential backends and browser-based login callback handling

L2.description: A reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where an error parameter from an identity provider's authentication callback response is rendered directly into an HTML page served to the user without proper sanitization or escaping. An attacker who can control or influence the error response can inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in the victim's browser, potentially allowing access to authentication tokens used in the Web UI.

L3.area: OIDC/JWT authentication credential backend's callback handling and HTML response generation
L3.description: A reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the OIDC authentication callback flow where error parameters from a failed authentication response are rendered directly into HTML error pages and script blocks without proper sanitization or escaping. When an OIDC role is configured with direct callback mode, an attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in the victim's browser, potentially allowing access to the Web UI authentication token.

ground_truth.reason: The OIDC callback handler reflects the error_description parameter from identity provider responses directly into HTML error pages and script blocks without escaping, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in the victim's browser.
